faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Does converting AVIF to PNG preserve transparency?
faq.a1: Yes! PNG fully supports the alpha channel. All transparent areas in your AVIF images will be perfectly preserved in the PNG output.
faq.a2: Yes, PNG uses lossless compression. Every pixel is preserved exactly as-is. This makes PNG ideal for graphics, logos, and screenshots where quality matters most.
faq.q3: Is it safe to convert private photos here?
faq.a3: Absolutely. Avifkit uses client-side processing technology. Your images are converted directly within your web browser memory and are never uploaded to any external server.
faq.q4: Why is PNG file size larger than AVIF?
faq.a4: PNG uses lossless compression while AVIF uses advanced lossy compression (AV1). AVIF files are typically 30-50% smaller than PNG at similar visual quality. The trade-off is that PNG preserves every single pixel perfectly.
faq.q5: Can I convert AVIF to PNG on mobile?
faq.a5: Yes! Avifkit works in any modern mobile browser (Chrome, Safari, Firefox). The conversion happens on your device, so no data is transferred over the network.
faq.q6: What is the maximum file size I can convert?
faq.a6: Each file can be up to 10MB. Since processing happens entirely in your browser, larger files may take slightly longer depending on your device's performance.
faq.q7: Can I batch convert multiple AVIF files?
faq.a7: Yes! Upload up to 30 AVIF files at once. Click Convert All, then download them individually or as a ZIP archive. All conversions run in parallel for maximum speed.
faq.q8: Should I use PNG or WebP for transparent images?
faq.a8: Both support transparency. PNG is universally compatible and lossless, making it the safe choice. WebP offers smaller file sizes but with slightly less software support. For web use, WebP is often preferred; for design work, use PNG.
faq.q9: Do I need to create an account to use Avifkit?
faq.a9: No. Avifkit is completely free with no registration, no watermarks, and no usage limits. Just open the page and start converting.
